summary: The test suite is comprehensive and well-structured, covering all major requirements: INI parsing (files and strings), comment handling, multi-line values, type coercion (int, float, bool, null, leading-zero strings, IPs), schema validation (missing keys/sections, type mismatches, open-world extras), JSON and YAML output (including file I/O), and end-to-end integration. Edge cases are thorough—duplicate keys/sections, orphan keys, quoted values with comment chars, immutability of coerce_types input, and case-insensitive booleans. Gaps that prevent a perfect rigor score: the CLI/main() function is entirely untested; there is no test for values containing multiple '=' signs (e.g., key = a=b=c); inline '#' comment stripping is not explicitly tested (only ';' is demonstrated); and malformed/completely unparseable lines have no direct assertion. Design is clean—logical TDD phases map to well-named classes, a fixture helper centralizes path management, float comparisons use pytest.approx, and tempfile cleanup is guarded with finally. Minor style issues: local per-function imports are idiomatic but unusual in pytest; several fixture files are opened without context managers (json.load(open(...))), which leaks file handles; and the fixture corpus (basic.ini, multiline.ini, etc.) is trusted to exist without a setup/teardown guard that would give a clear error if fixtures are missing.
